title: QUIC CRT client-side 1-RTT post-handshake ticket ingress architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest 1-RTT ingress seam: a client-role OneRtt CRYPTO path can recognize a TLS 1.3 NewSessionTicket message after Finished, extract opaque ticket bytes, and surface them through the existing internal update seam without claiming resumption, replay, or 0-RTT behavior.
risks: If the ingress path starts recognizing arbitrary post-handshake TLS messages, the slice will overgrow into a general TLS engine that the repository has not proven.
If the ingress path starts implying ticket persistence, replay ownership, PSK derivation, or 0-RTT behavior, the repository will overclaim capability it still does not own.
If pre-Finished or server-role ingress is allowed to survive into the retained ticket state, the slice will stop being a truthful client-only boundary.
design_summary: The managed bridge already owns the post-Finished opaque ticket seam through REQ-QUIC-CRT-0128. This slice adds the narrow OneRtt ingress path that can consume a real TLS 1.3 NewSessionTicket message after Finished, extract the opaque ticket bytes, and hand them to the same internal ticket update seam without changing handshake completion, application-data readiness, or the public surface. Unsupported 1-RTT post-handshake TLS messages are intentionally ignored rather than generalized into a broader post-handshake parser.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only client-role 1-RTT CRYPTO ingress for a valid TLS 1.3 NewSessionTicket message after Finished. It does not add a general post-handshake TLS parser, a replay cache, a resumption story, or a public API expansion, and pre-Finished or server-role ingress remains rejected.
